# UC-21.10.9 · Claims Leakage and Overpayment Detection

## Description

Payments and reserves roll up per claim, claimant type, and adjuster: the search compares cumulative paid amounts to latest reserves and stated coverage limits and adds a statistical tail score within each claim type to highlight unusually heavy payouts versus peer claims.

## Value

Claims leadership and finance protect combined ratio and reserves by spotting duplicate or excessive settlements, reserve erosion, or limit-adjacent payments early enough for special investigation or recovery before leakage becomes material in quarterly results.

## Implementation

Filter closed claims appropriately if reserves zero out at closure. Exclude subrogation recoveries unless netted into `payment_amount`. Use claim-type strata that mirror actuarial groupings so `avg_paid` and `stdev` are meaningful. **Domain context:** catastrophe codes and reinsurance treaties can legitimate high paid-to-reserve movement—maintain peril tags. **Splunk:** Protect against divide-by-zero with `eval` wrappers when reserves are null or zero.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• JDBC or API extract into `index=claims` with stable `claim_id`, split sourcetypes or a type field distinguishing payment vs reserve rows, and policy limit sourced from underwriting—PII masked per governance.
• Actuarial-approved claim-type taxonomy for `eventstats` buckets; minimum volume per type before trusting z-score.
• SIU playbook reference for escalation when analytic hits occur.

Create search
• Normalize currency and sign conventions (payments positive, recoveries negative) before `stats sum`.
• Add `eval` guards: `current_reserve = if(current_reserve<=0,null(),current_reserve)` and similar for denominators.
• Save as correlation search `claims_leakage_overpayment`; tune thresholds with last four closed quarters.

Understanding this SPL
Rows summarize financial movement per claim. Ratios relate paid dollars to reserves and limits. Peer comparison within `claim_type` yields a z-score; the `where` clause unions ratio breaches with statistical outliers. Results sort by descending z-score so the most anomalous financial profiles surface first.

Validate
• Reconcile ten flagged claims against the adjuster workstation and banking detail for totals and timestamps.
• Confirm `coverage_limit` matches policy declarations for sampled commercial multi-line exposures.
• Compare distribution of z-scores to actuarial loss triangles for reasonableness by line.

Operationalize
• Route hits to SIU queues with claim deep link and prior payment chronology screenshot instructions.
• Weekly leadership digest with trending counts by peril and region; stale reserve refresh SLA tracked separately.
• Troubleshooting: null z-score implies zero variance tiny groups—fallback to percentile rank; sudden drop in alerts may indicate missing payment feed after a connector upgrade.

## SPL

```spl
index=claims sourcetype IN ("claims:payment","claims:reserve")
| stats sum(payment_amount) as total_paid, latest(reserve_amount) as current_reserve, latest(policy_limit) as coverage_limit by claim_id, claim_type, adjuster_id
| eval paid_vs_reserve=round((total_paid/current_reserve)*100, 1), paid_vs_limit=round((total_paid/coverage_limit)*100, 1)
| eventstats avg(total_paid) as avg_paid, stdev(total_paid) as sd_paid by claim_type
| eval zscore=round((total_paid-avg_paid)/sd_paid, 2)
| where paid_vs_reserve > 110 OR zscore > 2.5 OR paid_vs_limit > 95
| sort -zscore
```

## Visualization

Table (claim_id, claim_type, adjuster_id, total_paid, paid_vs_reserve, paid_vs_limit, zscore), Bar chart (top claims by total_paid), Scatter (paid_vs_reserve vs zscore), Single value (claims breaching thresholds).

## Known False Positives

Fast-developing liability claims can legitimately exceed early reserves before adjusters refresh them, spiking paid-to-reserve until the next reserve evaluation cycle. Large medical claims with multiple partial payments may sum past an interim reserve that was always meant to grow. Company-wide fee schedule changes move the `avg_paid` baseline and can push benign claims into the tail until history repopulates. Duplicate rows in the feed from replayed extracts mimic double payment until deduplication keys are fixed.
